replication_study.hypothesis: Among participants who pass the preregistered exclusion criteria, the interaction between self-concept clarity (Clear = 1 vs. Conflicted = -1) and rating-distribution shape (Bimodal = 1 vs. Unimodal = -1) will significantly predict product desirability (average of likelihood to watch, perceived quality, and purchase likelihood). Specifically, the regression coefficient for the clarity × rating interaction will be negative (crossover pattern).

data_plan.source_type: Online survey experiment hosted on formr.io with crowdsourced participants
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single data-collection wave; all observations with Exclusion == 0 are retained
data_plan.sample_size: ≈ 376 participants after exclusion filtering (out of 898 total records)
data_plan.access_details: Openly provided in the replication package at /app/data/original/29/input/replication_data/SCORE_ALL_DATA.csv

qualification.explanation: The dataset contains the same experimental manipulations (bimodal vs. unimodal ratings; clear vs. conflicted self-concept clarity) and the same outcome items as the original paper, but was collected independently from the original authors, so it provides an appropriate independent test of the claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: • Variables TYPE, S1_likely, S1_quality, S1_purchase represent the same manipulations and desirability items described in the original experiments (post_registration.json → method.description; SCORE Data dictionary .pdf pages 2-4).  • Coding scheme for Experimental (1 = Bimodal, ‑1 = Unimodal) and Clarity (1 = Clear, ‑1 = Conflicted) exactly matches the contrasts reported in the original Study 2a coefficient (initial_details.txt, post_registration.json → results.numerical_results[1]).
qualification.deviation_from_original: • Participants in this SCORE dataset were recruited later and via formr rather than MTurk/SSI (SCORE Data dictionary .pdf: description of tabs; post_registration.json → data.source).  • Larger raw N = 898 and cleaned N ≈ 376 vs. Study 2a N = 149 (post_registration.json → data.sample_size).  • Single combined study instead of eight separate studies.
qualification.notes: The raw file includes non-numeric codings and multiple exclusion reasons; analysis script applies the preregistered Exclusion == 0 rule.

data_plan.notes: Variable names contain spaces (e.g., “SCL compliant code ”); code handles this via pandas. Missing values are encoded as blank cells; treated with pandas to_numeric(errors='coerce').

planned_method.steps:
1. Load SCORE_ALL_DATA.csv from /app/data.
2. Filter rows with Exclusion == 0 (preregistered usable cases).
3. Compute desirability = mean(S1_likely, S1_quality, S1_purchase) per participant.
4. Contrast-code predictors: experimental = 1 (Bimodal) / ‑1 (Unimodal); clarity = 1 (Clear) / ‑1 (Conflicted) based on TYPE string; interaction = experimental * clarity.
5. Fit OLS regression: desirability ~ experimental + clarity + interaction.
6. Extract coefficient, SE, t, and p for the interaction term.
7. Save summary table to /app/data/replication_results.csv and print to console.
planned_method.models: Ordinary Least Squares (linear regression) with contrast-coded predictors
planned_method.independent_variables: experimental (rating distribution shape), clarity (self-concept clarity), and their interaction
planned_method.control_variables: None (main effects included in the model satisfy orthogonality requirements)

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Regression coefficient for the interaction term
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test on the interaction coefficient (two-tailed, α = 0.05)

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for rows with any NA in S1_likely, S1_quality, S1_purchase
planned_method.inference_criteria: Reject H0 if p < 0.05 and coefficient sign is negative; compare magnitude to original b ≈ -0.17

files.replicate_analysis.py: Loads SCORE_ALL_DATA, applies exclusion rule, constructs variables, runs OLS regression, outputs regression summary and CSV of coefficients.

codebase.notes: All file paths are hard-coded to /app/data; script is self-contained and requires only common Python scientific packages.

analysis.instructions: From the project root (inside docker), run:  python /app/data/original/29/input/replication_data/replicate_analysis.py
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction and statistical significance (p-value) of the clarity × rating interaction coefficient; magnitude comparison to original coefficient (b = -0.17, SE = 0.08).
